title: 10 best herbal teas for eye swelling
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye swelling are infusions made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce eye puffiness and discomfort. Chamomile, for instance, contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that can alleviate eye swelling and irritation. Similarly, calendula tea has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye inflammation and promote healing. By drinking these herbal teas or using them as compresses, individuals can experience relief from eye swelling, leading to improved vision and overall well-being. For example, a person who spends long hours in front of a computer may find relief from eye strain and puffiness by drinking chamomile tea or applying calendula tea compresses.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eye area. The herb, Euphrasia officinalis, is rich in bioflavonoids, rosmarinic acid, and other active compounds that have a soothing effect on the eyes. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling caused by allergies, conjunctivitis, or other conditions. The tea's natural properties help to tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and providing a refreshing, rejuvenating sensation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory property is important for eye swelling because it helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the eye area, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils.
Astringent property: Eyebright tea's astringent property is crucial for eye swelling as it helps constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, due to the presence of tannins and saponins.

Antihistamine property: Eyebright tea's antihistamine property is significant for eye swelling because it helps alleviate allergic reactions that can cause swelling, thanks to its anti-allergenic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Analgesic property: Eyebright tea's analgesic property is important for eye swelling because it helps relieve pain and discomfort associated with swelling, which can be attributed to the tea's active constituents like phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial property: Eyebright tea's antimicrobial property is significant for eye swelling because it helps prevent infections that can lead to swelling, due to the tea's antibacterial and antiviral properties.
remedy_recipe: In a cup, combine 1 teaspoon of dried eyebright herb and 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb in the water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the herb from the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea, slowly blinking the eyes to allow the liquid to reach the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Eyebright flowers are commonly used in medicinal teas for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the eyebright plant are rich in tannins and flavonoids, making them effective in reducing eye swelling.
Seeds: Eyebright seeds contain potent antioxidants that help alleviate eye swelling and promote eye health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The tea is made from the leaves of the plantain plant, which are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and saponins that can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and puffiness around the eyes. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a compress can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling caused by allergies, fatigue, or infection. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids and tannins.
Antihistamine: Plantain tea can alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, as it contains antihistamine properties that counteract allergic reactions.
Astringent: The astringent properties of plantain tea, due to tannins and polyphenols, help tighten and constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid buildup and swelling around the eyes.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tea's antimicrobial properties, from compounds like allantoin and tannins, help prevent and treat eye infections that may cause swelling.
Analgesic: Plantain tea offers pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate discomfort associated with eye swelling, thanks to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a healthy, pesticide-free area.
Rinse leaves thoroughly, then tear into small pieces and place in a pot.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat, simmer for 15 minutes, then strain and let the tea cool.
Store tea in a covered container in the refrigerator for up to one week. To use, soak two cotton balls in the tea and place over closed eyes for 15 minutes, three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain plant are used in medicinal teas for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Plantain seeds are rich in mucilage, making them effective in soothing irritated eyes and reducing swelling.
Flowers: Plantain flowers have been used in traditional medicine for reducing eye inflammation and swelling due to their antioxidant properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that have antioxidant effects. These compounds help to protect the eyes from damage and reduce inflammation, making pot marigold tea an effective natural remedy for eye swelling. The tea can be applied to the eyes using a compress, allowing the active compounds to be absorbed directly into the skin around the eyes. This can help to reduce puffiness, dark circles, and other symptoms of eye swelling, providing relief and promoting healthy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea reduces eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help prevent eye infections that can cause swelling, due to the active constituents like safranal and quercetin.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea's antioxidant effects protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, minimizing swelling, as a result of the high levels of antioxidants such as lutein and zeaxanthin.
Astringent: The astringent properties of pot marigold tea help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing swelling, by the action of tannins present in the plant.

Circulation-boosting: Pot marigold tea promotes blood circulation, which helps decrease eye swelling by reducing the buildup of fluid and promoting the drainage of toxins, due to the active constituents like flavonoids and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, place 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the flowers.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory flavonoids, making them ideal for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold contain calendulin, a substance with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye swelling.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and pain. The tea is rich in antioxidants, including terpenoids and flavonoids, which can help reduce redness and puffiness in the eyes. Additionally, the tea's soothing and cooling effects can help alleviate discomfort and irritation caused by swelling. To use chamomile tea for eye swelling, simply soak two tea bags in hot water for a few minutes, then let them cool down. Once cooled, place the tea bags on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process several times a day until the swelling subsides.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the active constituent chamazulene, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Antioxidant: The high levels of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, in chamomile tea can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce swelling.
Antimicrobial: German chamomile tea has antimicrobial properties due to its azulene content, which can help prevent eye infections and reduce swelling caused by infection.
Astringent: Chamomile tea has astringent properties, which can help tighten the skin around the eyes and reduce puffiness and swelling.
Analgesic: German chamomile tea contains analgesic compounds, such as matricin and apigenin, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried German chamomile flowers in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the chamomile flowers from the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton balls in the cooled chamomile tea and place them on closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers are commonly used to reduce eye swelling and irritation.
Leaves: Containing soothing agents, chamomile leaves help alleviate irritation and decrease puffiness in eye swelling.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants, chamomile seeds can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in eye swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with eye swelling because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These components can help reduce inflammation and puffiness in the eye area. White tea also contains tannins and catechins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of swelling. Additionally, white tea has antimicrobial and antiviral properties that can help protect the eyes from infection and irritation. Drinking white tea or using it as a compress can provide relief and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce eye swelling by minimizing irritation and redness, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in white tea, particularly catechins, combats free radicals that cause eye puffiness and dark circles.
Antihistamine: White tea's natural antihistamine properties help alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, as it contains flavonoids that inhibit histamine release.
Astringent: White tea's astringent properties, attributed to tannins, help tighten the skin around the eyes and reduce puffiness.
Vasoconstrictor: White tea contains vasoconstrictor compounds that help constrict blood vessels around the eyes, reducing swelling and redness.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool to 175-185°F (79-85°C).
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried white tea leaves in the hot water for 1-3 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak 2 cotton balls in the cooled white tea.
Place the tea-soaked cotton balls on closed eyes for 15-20 minutes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves can help reduce eye inflammation and swelling.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory properties, white tea buds can soothe irritated eyes and reduce swelling.
Flowers: Containing potent antioxidants, white tea flowers can help alleviate eye puffiness and promote healthy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the body. The tea is rich in antioxidants and vitamin C, which can help to strengthen the blood vessels around the eyes and reduce the appearance of puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, the cooling effect of the tea can provide immediate relief to irritated and swollen eyes, making them look more refreshed and rejuvenated. Drinking lime tea regularly can also help to boost the immune system and improve overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lime tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its citrus bioflavonoids, helps reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation and irritation.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including vitamin C, found in lime tea, aids in minimizing eye swelling by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting healing.
Astringent: Lime tea's astringent properties, derived from tannins, assist in alleviating eye swelling by constricting blood vessels and reducing redness.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of lime tea, attributed to its limonene content, helps with eye swelling by providing pain relief and soothing discomfort.

Antimicrobial: Lime tea's antimicrobial property, due to its limonene and linalool constituents, contributes to reducing eye swelling by combating potential infections and preventing further irritation.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried lime flowers in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the mixture and steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the tea and place them on closed eyes for 15 minutes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and antibacterial properties, lime leaves can help reduce eye swelling and irritation.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, lime flowers can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling and promote healing.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants, lime seeds can help decrease eye swelling and soothe irritated eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with eye swelling because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling. The tea can be used as a compress and applied to the eyes to soothe irritation and reduce puffiness. Additionally, witch hazel has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling and promote healing. Drinking witch hazel tea can also provide internal benefits, as it can help reduce inflammation in the body and support overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea reduces inflammation and swelling around the eyes, thanks to its high tannin content.
Astringent: It tightens the skin and reduces puffiness due to its astringent properties, mainly attributed to its gallic acid and ellagic acid content.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea helps protect the skin around the eyes from damage and inflammation caused by free radicals, due to its rich antioxidant content, particularly flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.
Soothing: Witch hazel tea soothes irritated skin and helps alleviate discomfort, as a result of its anti-inflammatory and tannin constituents.
Vasoconstrictor: It helps reduce redness and swelling by constricting blood vessels, owing to its gallic acid and theaflavin-3,3'-digallate content.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 2 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Place 2 teaspoons of dried witch hazel herb in a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean glass container and allow it to cool.
Soak two cotton balls in the cooled tea, then place them over closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have a soothing effect on irritated eyes and can help reduce swelling.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain phenolic acids and tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce eye inflammation and swelling.
Seeds: The seeds of the witch hazel plant contain high levels of oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye swelling.
Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant contain active compounds such as gallic acid and tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce eye swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the yarrow plant, which are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids. These compounds have been shown to have a soothing effect on the eyes, reducing puffiness and redness. Additionally, yarrow tea has antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help to prevent infection and reduce excess fluid in the eye area. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from eye swelling and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation and irritation.
Astringent: The astringent property of yarrow tea, caused by tannins, is important for eye swelling as it helps constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid leakage, thus alleviating puffiness.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial property, owing to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and camphor, is crucial for eye swelling as it helps prevent and treat potential infections causing eye inflammation.
Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic property, resulting from the presence of alkaloids and glycosides, is important for eye swelling as it helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swelling.
Diuretic: Yarrow tea's diuretic property, due to the presence of lactones and flavonoids, is significant for eye swelling as it promotes fluid excretion, reducing eye puffiness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried yarrow herb.
Place the herb in a tea infuser or empty teabag.
Boil eight ounces of water and let it cool for two minutes.
Pour the hot water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for five to ten minutes.
Remove the infuser or teabag and discard the herb. Let the tea cool before using it as a compress for eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of yarrow have been used in teas to alleviate eye swelling due to their soothing properties.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are rich in antioxidants that can help decrease inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Stems: Yarrow stems contain compounds that can help reduce eye swelling and irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The tea is rich in antioxidants and bioflavonoids that help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and strengthen capillary walls. Drinking maidenhair tree tea or using it as a compress can help alleviate eye swelling, dark circles, and eye bags, giving you a refreshed and rejuvenated look. The tea's soothing and calming effects also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to eye swelling and other skin problems.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree tea reduces eye swelling by inhibiting the production of inflammatory compounds, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antioxidant: The tea's potent antioxidants, such as catechins and tannins, protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can cause eye swelling.
Astringent: Maidenhair tree tea's astringent properties, due to its tannin content, help reduce eye swelling by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention.
Antihistamine: The tea's antihistamine effects, attributed to its flavonoid content, help relieve eye swelling caused by allergies.
Analgesic: Maidenhair tree tea's analgesic properties, resulting from the presence of triterpenes, alleviate eye swelling and provide relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried maidenhair tree leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the tea simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove leaves and let it cool down.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea daily for eye swelling relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye swelling.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants that can soothe irritated eyes and decrease swelling.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate eye swelling and infections.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom tea helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as ruscogenins, have been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. When consumed as a tea, butcher's broom can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage and irritation, further reducing the appearance of eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butcher's broom tea reduces eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, due to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and alkaloids.
Venotonic: It helps alleviate eye swelling by improving blood flow and reducing capillary permeability, thanks to its ruscogenin and neuroscoegenin content.
Antioxidant: Butcher's broom tea combats eye swelling by neutralizing free radicals that cause tissue damage, as it contains various antioxidant compounds.
Diuretic: It reduces eye swelling by promoting fluid elimination, due to its active diuretic constituents, primarily ruscogenin.
Vasoconstrictor: Butcher's broom tea helps with eye swelling by narrowing blood vessels, which reduces blood flow and swelling, owing to its ruscogenin and neuroscoegenin content.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried butcher's broom herb.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid herb remnants.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the prepared butcher's broom tea, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help reduce eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of butcher's broom are commonly used in medicinal teas for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of butcher's broom contain active compounds that help reduce swelling and promote circulation, making them useful for eye swelling.
Seeds: The seeds of butcher's broom are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas for eye swelling.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye swelling with herbal teas is to apply warm compresses soaked in cooled tea. Choose teas with anti-inflammatory properties, such as chamomile or green tea. Steep the tea in boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then let it cool. Soak a clean cloth in the tea and place it over closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties will help reduce swelling and soothe the area around the eyes. However, if swelling persists or is accompanied by other symptoms, it is important to seek medical attention.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal teas are typically related to inflammation or irritation of the eye area. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and redness around the eyes. Similarly, green tea contains bioflavonoids that can alleviate eye irritation and inflammation. Additionally, fennel tea can help soothe eye strain and fatigue, while calendula tea has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can treat eye infections and swelling. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment, as they can interact with medications or exacerbate certain medical conditions.
